parameters.name: accept
parameters.description: Specifies the format of the response from the server, should be application/json.

mode.description: Mode of generating video, optional, enumeration value: std (High performance) or pro (High performance). Default value is std.

keep_original_sound.description: Whether to keep the original sound, optional, enumeration value: yes (keep) or no (remove). Default value is yes.

image_url.description: Reference images: The characters, backgrounds and other elements in the generated video have all been based on the reference images.

video_url.description: Refer to the link for obtaining the video. The character movements in the generated video are consistent with those in the reference video.

character_orientation.description: The orientation of the characters in the generated video can be chosen to be consistent with the picture or the video.

prompt.example: White ceramic coffee mug on glossy marble countertop with morning window light. Camera slowly rotates 360 degrees around the mug, pausing briefly at the handle.
prompt.description: Text prompt words can contain both positive and negative descriptions.

callback_url.description: The callback URL which will be called when the video is generated

id.description: The ID of the kling task.

request.description: The request of the kling task.

response.description: The response of the kling task.

parameters.name: accept
parameters.description: Specifies the format of the response from the server, should be application/json.

mode.description: Mode of generating video, optional. Supported values: `std` (High performance), `pro` (High quality), `4k` (Native 4K, only supported by `kling-v3` and `kling-v3-omni`; not compatible with `motion control`). Default value is `std`.

model.description: The model used to generate the videos, default value is kling-v1

action.description: The action to generate the video. If `text2video`, the video will be generated from the prompt.

prompt.example: White ceramic coffee mug on glossy marble countertop with morning window light. Camera slowly rotates 360 degrees around the mug, pausing briefly at the handle.
prompt.description: The prompt which used to generate the video.

duration.description: Video generation duration, in seconds, optional. For kling-v3/kling-v3-omni: flexible 3-15 seconds (integer). For other models: enumeration value 5 or 10. Requests that include endframes only support 5 seconds.

generate_audio.description: Whether to generate audio along with the video, optional. Supported only by `kling-v3`, `kling-v3-omni`, and `kling-v2-6` (pro mode only). Other models will reject this flag if set to true. Default is false.

video_id.description: When the action is extend, you can specify video_id to extend the video.

cfg_scale.description: Degree of freedom to generate video, optional. The greater the value, the stronger the correlation. Value range: [0,1].

aspect_ratio.description: The aspect ratio of the video, optional, enumeration value: 16:9, 9:16, 1:1. Default value is 16:9.

callback_url.description: The callback URL which will be called when the video is generated

end_image_url.description: End-frame reference URL. Only valid with `action=image2video` and a non-empty `start_image_url`. Model/mode constraints (mirrors Kling docs): `kling-v1` requires `duration=5`; `kling-v1-6`/`kling-v2-5-turbo`/`kling-v2-6` require `mode=pro`; `kling-v3`/`kling-v3-omni`/`kling-video-o1` accept end-frame in all modes; `kling-v2-master`/`kling-v2-1-master` do not support end-frame.

camera_control.description: Contains 6 Fields, used to specify the camera's movement or change in different directions. camera_control.type should be one of 'simple', 'down_back', 'forward_up', 'left_turn_forward', 'right_turn_forward'. camera_control.config is an optional object, contains 6 optional fields - camera_control.config.horizontal, camera_control.config.vertical, camera_control.config.pan, camera_control.config.tilt, camera_control.config.roll, camera_control.config.zoom. The value of each field is a number, and the value range is [-1,1].

element_list.description: This parameter is used to specify a list of reference subjects when generating content, configured in the `element_list` array using `element_id` (the ID from the subject library). The number of reference subjects must be counted together with the number of reference images under the following limits: **if there is a reference video, reference subjects + reference images ≤ 4**; **if there is no reference video, reference subjects + reference images ≤ 7**.

video_list.description: This parameter is used to upload a reference video via `video_url` (MP4/MOV format, duration between 3–10 seconds, resolution between 720px and 2160px, frame rate between 24–60fps, file size ≤200MB, and only 1 video allowed). You can set the `refer_type` to **feature** (feature reference video) or **base** (video to be edited, default); videos set as **base** cannot define start or end frames. You can use `keep_original_sound` to choose whether to retain the original audio (`yes` to keep, `no` to remove, applicable to both types). The `video_url` must not be empty.

negative_prompt.description: The value is optional and contains a maximum of 200 characters.

start_image_url.description: You can specify the first frame to generate video, when the action is image2video.
